name: Tilt (Kubernetes Local Development)

auth.notes: Local tool; uses kubeconfig for cluster access

performance.notes: Incremental builds fast; initial build time depends on image complexity

agent_readiness.notes: Local Kubernetes development with live-updating. Tiltfile (Starlark Python-like). docker_build('my-image', '.', live_update=[...]) + k8s_resource('deployment'). Live update: sync files to running container without rebuild. Web UI for service status, logs, build history. local_resource() for non-k8s tasks. k8s_yaml(). Works with kind/minikube/OrbStack. Smart rebuild: only affected services. Competes with Skaffold (simpler YAML config).

gotchas: Tiltfile uses Starlark (Python subset) — learning curve
Docker acquired Tilt in 2022 — future uncertain
Web UI port 10350 needs to be open
Complex multi-service setups can have subtle rebuild ordering issues
best_when: Kubernetes microservices local development with fast iteration
Teams wanting live file sync without container rebuilds
Web UI visibility into service health and build logs
avoid_when: When Skaffold YAML config preferred over Starlark
summary: Tilt enables fast local Kubernetes development with live-updating. Tiltfile (Starlark) defines docker_build + k8s_resource. Live update syncs files to running containers without rebuild. Web UI at localhost:10350 for service status and logs. Works with kind/minikube/OrbStack. Smart incremental rebuilds. Alternative to Skaffold with richer UI and live-update capabilities.
